mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW M8 engine typically costs £8,000–£12,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — the S63 in both standard and Competition form. Fitting adds £650–£2,100. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M8 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S63B44T4 in the M8 Competition — the latest iteration of the S63 with improvements over earlier versions. The S63 is a proven engine, but oil leaks, turbocharger failures, and cooling system weaknesses are well-documented.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M8 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but an early S63 (2019–2020) without documented oil leak repair history, turbocharger inspections, or comprehensive warranty is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The engine is proven; the known issues are the problem.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my M8 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By engine: oil drips (S63 oil leaks), wastegate rattle (S63 turbocharger failure), coolant loss (S63 cooling system weakness), smoke from tailpipe on startup (S63 valve stem seal wear). Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £8,000–£12,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M8 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M8's values are strong enough to justify engine work. Against a £100,000+ new M8, an £8,000–£12,000+ S63 recon is 8–12% — ✅ safe. Against a £40,000 used M8, the same recon is 20–30% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M8 should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: M8 Competition — 625 hp, the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car, and the ultimate BMW M car experience. For the most practical option, the Gran Coupé (F93) offers four-door practicality. For the open-top experience, the Convertible (F91) is the ultimate grand tourer. Buy the newest you can afford, always with a comprehensive warranty.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your variant (M8, M8 Competition, M8 Competition Gran Coupé) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW M8 vs M5 — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share the S63 engine — the S63 in the M8 and F90 M5 are identical. The M8 is heavier and more luxurious; the M5 is more practical. Reliability is identical; the M8 works the powertrain harder due to its weight and power output. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The M5 offers more practicality; the M8 offers more style and presence.

hero.h1: M8 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW M8 engine across the flagship grand tourer range — from the S63 twin-turbo V8 in the standard M8 to the 625 hp Competition variant. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's ultimate M car. Backed by real UK M8 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The M8 rule — flagship power, flagship bills: an S63 recon at £8,000–£12,000+ against a £100,000+ new M8 is 8–12% — ✅ safe. Against a £40,000 used M8, the same recon is 20–30% — ⚠️ watch. The M8's values hold strong, but the S63's known issues mean buying without a warranty is a calculated risk.
★★★★☆ — the S63 is a proven twin-turbo V8 shared with the F90 M5, but oil leaks, turbocharger issues, and cooling system weaknesses are well-documented

The engine is proven and powerful, but oil leaks, turbocharger failures, and cooling system weaknesses are the known risks. Buy the newest Competition you can afford — and always with a warranty.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW M8 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW M8 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: S63B44T4 rating: 85% confidence — the S63 is well-documented across the M range (M5 F10/F90, M6 F06/F12/F13, X5M/X6M) with clear patterns from specialist rebuilders. The S63 is a proven engine, but oil leaks, turbocharger failures, and cooling system weaknesses are well-documented. The M8-specific S63B44T4 is the latest iteration with improvements over earlier versions. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The M8 uses the S63B44T4 engine — a 4.4L twin-turbo V8 shared with the F90 M5. The engine code is S63B44T4, not S63B44B as seen in some sources. Correction applied: The S63 engine produces 591 hp in standard M8 form and 625 hp in Competition form. The Competition variant is the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car. Correction applied: The M8 was produced from July 2019 to October 2025. The Gran Coupé (F93) was added to the lineup in October 2019.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the M8 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: One of the most prevalent issues with the S63 engine — oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and the oil filter housing. Affects all S63-powered M8 models. £1,500–£3,000 repair if caught early; engine damage if ignored.

The S63's twin turbos operate under high stress. Oil starvation or contamination can lead to turbocharger failures. Wastegate rattle is a common symptom. Affects all S63-powered M8 models. Turbo replacement: £3,000–£6,000.
Coolant leaks from the expansion tank due to chemical/thermal reaction with the plastic material. Affects all S63-powered M8 models. £400–£800 repair if caught early.
The S63 engine can experience high oil consumption at higher mileages. Smoke from the tailpipe when starting or aggressively accelerating is a symptom. Regular oil level monitoring is essential.

problems.issue: Early Model Valve Stem Seal Wear
problems.description: Early S63 engines experienced valve stem seal wear. Early M8 models (2019–2020) are the primary concern. Smoke from the tailpipe on startup is the classic symptom.
Not an engine failure — but common non-engine M8 enquiries. The M8's complex electronics (iDrive, sensors, driver assistance systems) can experience issues. Check all systems on any used M8.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW M8 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the M8 is a modern classic [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — the Competition is the most popular variant [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The M8 generates a different kind of enquiry than the rest of the BMW range — owners are high-net-worth enthusiasts doing due diligence on specific known issues (S63 oil leaks, turbocharger failures) rather than families facing unexpected failures. The M8 buyer knows what they're getting into — they just want to know which variant to choose and what to look for. The Competition is the overwhelming favourite — 625 hp, the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The M8 Competition is the sweet spot in the used market — 625 hp, the S63 engine, proven and powerful, and depreciated into more accessible territory (£40,000–£70,000). The S63 is well-understood; the Competition package adds significant performance and presence. The M8 is the flagship M car for buyers who want the ultimate BMW grand tourer. (Editorial synthesis.)

The M8 is a rare sight on UK roads — but it's a future classic. The S63 engine is the last of the pure V8 M engines before hybridisation and electrification take over. The M8 is the M car for collectors and enthusiasts who want the ultimate expression of BMW M power. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW M8 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one M8 based on this page, buy an M8 Competition with the S63 — it's the sweet spot in the range: 625 hp, the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car, and depreciated into more accessible territory. Buy the newest you can afford, always with a comprehensive warranty, and budget for S63 oil leak and turbocharger inspections before you buy — it's the smart money on any M8. BMW M8 — SECTIONS 8-14

replacementCosts.h2: BMW M8 Engine Replacement Costs — By Model
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW M8 engine typically takes 18–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The M8 rule — flagship power, flagship bills: An £8,000–£12,000+ S63 recon against a £100,000+ new M8 sits at 8–12% — ✅ safe. Against a £40,000 used M8, the same recon is 20–30% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Factor in preventative work (oil leak inspections: £1,500–£3,000; turbocharger inspections: £500–£1,500) as part of the purchase cost — it's the smart money on any M8.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW M8 engine typically takes 18–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The M8 rule — flagship power, flagship bills: An £8,000–£12,000+ S63 recon against a £100,000+ new M8 sits at 8–12% — ✅ safe. Against a £40,000 used M8, the same recon is 20–30% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Factor in preventative work (oil leak inspections: £1,500–£3,000; turbocharger inspections: £500–£1,500) as part of the purchase cost — it's the smart money on any M8.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW M8 Engine Range Evolved — 2019 to 2025

eras.era: M8 launch — the flagship M car
eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the M8 as the flagship grand tourer — the most powerful and luxurious M model ever. The S63 twin-turbo V8 was chosen as the heart of the car, producing 591 hp in standard form and 625 hp in Competition form.
eras.worthKnowing: The M8 shares its S63 engine with the F90 M5, X5M and X6M. The Competition variant is the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The M8 Competition was launched alongside the standard M8, delivering 625 hp — the most powerful BMW M engine ever in a standard production car. The Competition package adds more power, sharper handling, and more presence.
eras.worthKnowing: The M8 Competition is the M8 to buy — 625 hp, 3.2s to 62 mph, and the ultimate BMW M car experience.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The Gran Coupé (F93) was added to the lineup, offering four-door practicality with the same S63 engine. The Gran Coupé is the most practical M8 and the most popular variant.
eras.worthKnowing: The Gran Coupé is the M8 for buyers who want flagship performance with four-door practicality.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW M8?

profiles.verdict: The M8 is built for this — the Competition variant delivers 625 hp, wafting comfort, and the perfect balance of luxury and performance. The S63 is proven and powerful; the chassis is magnificent. The M8 Competition is the ultimate grand tourer.

The M8 is a GT, not a track car — but 625 hp and a twin-turbo V8 means it's devastatingly fast. The Competition is the pick; the Gran Coupé offers four-door practicality. The M8 is the ultimate expression of BMW M power.

The M8 is a future classic — the last of the pure V8 M cars before hybridisation and electrification take over. The Competition is the one to buy; the Gran Coupé is the most practical. Both are worth proper investment.

A £40,000 used M8 is not a cheap M8 — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £60,000 M8 Competition with a warranty is the bargain; a £40,000 example without a warranty is the trap.
The M8 Gran Coupé is one of the most beautiful BMWs ever made — four-door coupe styling, frameless doors, and a sloping roofline. The Convertible (F91) is the ultimate open-top grand tourer.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW M8 Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Oil leaks from an S63? Turbocharger issues on a Competition? On the M8, the answer is almost always repair — the values are strong enough to justify engine work, especially on the Competition. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
